events.label: reasoning
events.text: The scenario involves fall protection equipment with frayed/burned webbing. This is clearly non-compliant - damaged webbing on fall protection equipment must be removed from service immediately. OSHA requires fall protection equipment to be inspected and damaged equipment taken out of service.

  "findings": [
    "Frayed/burned webbing on fall protection equipment is damaged and cannot be used",
    "Damaged webbing compromises load-bearing capacity and may fail under fall arrest forces"
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Immediately remove damaged harness/lanyard/webbing from service and tag out",
    "Replace with new, inspected, compliant equipment before any work at height proceeds",
    "Inspect all other fall protection equipment on site for similar damage",
    "Document defect and notify safety officer"
  ],
  "rationale": "Frayed or burned webbing on fall protection equipment is explicitly defective under OSHA requirements. Personal fall arrest systems must be inspected prior to each use and damaged equipment must be removed from service immediately. Burned or frayed webbing significantly reduces the strength of the strap, creating a critical risk of catastrophic failure during a fall event.",
  "references": [
    "OSHA 1926 Subpart M - Fall Protection",
    "OSHA 1910.140 - Personal Fall Protection Systems",
    "1926.502(d)(21) - Personal fall arrest systems shall be inspected prior to each use for wear, damage and other deterioration",
    "1910.140(c)(18) - Personal fall protection equipment must be inspected before use"
  ]
}
